summary: Full-stack test for Ride N' Nice landing page. Backend: 6/6 pytest cases pass (root, /events shape, /join valid create, invalid email 422, missing required 422, list persistence with no _id leak and desc sort). Frontend: all required data-testids present (hero, nav, about-stats, routes carousel with 5 cards + prev/next + 5 dots, 5 gallery tiles, 4 events timeline rows, 4 social links, join form with all inputs + submit, footer, mobile nav toggle/panel/5 links/cta). Valid join submit correctly transitions button label to 'BIENVENIDO A LA CREW'. Invalid email is blocked by native HTML5 type=email validation (no crash, native browser error). Fixed one minor issue: hero-marquee data-testid did not reach DOM because react-fast-marquee does not forward the prop; moved data-testid to wrapping div.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logger is defined AFTER it is referenced inside create_join_request exception handler. On insert failure this will raise NameError instead of returning 500. Move logging.basicConfig + logger = getLogger(__name__) to the top of the file (before route definitions).
Join.jsx uses custom prop `testid` on <Field/>; ensure Field component maps it to data-testid (works today). Consider standardizing to data-testid end-to-end.

Join form relies on HTML5 type=email for client validation; server still validates via EmailStr. Consider a user-friendly inline error for invalid email instead of browser-native bubble for consistency.
seed_data_creation: Two TEST_ prefixed join requests created via /api/join during pytest. Non-destructive; not cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend endpoints: GET /api/, GET /api/events, POST/GET /api/join are all working. If re-running backend suite twice, the list test remains valid. If Hero marquee testid is ever modified back onto the Marquee component directly, it will not be forwarded to DOM (react-fast-marquee drops unknown props); keep wrapping div with the testid.
rca_of_the_issue: hero-marquee missing: root cause is react-fast-marquee not forwarding arbitrary HTML data-* props to the rendered container. Fix: put data-testid='hero-marquee' on the outer wrapper div in Hero.jsx instead of the <Marquee/> component.
